records.ety: [OE. plainte, pleynte, F. plainte, fr. L. plangere, planctum (plancta, fem. p. p.), to beat, beat the breast, lament. Cf. Complain, Plague, Plangent.]

defs: Audible expression of sorrow; lamentation; complaint; hence, a mournful song; a lament.
An accusation or protest on account of an injury.
quotes: There are three just grounds of war with Spain: one of plaint, two upon defense.

defs: A private memorial tendered to a court, in which a person sets forth his cause of action; the exhibiting of an action in writing.
